description: Visual dashboard and whiteboard for managing Harness SDD projects.

features.name: webview-foundation
features.title: Foundation & Webview Communication
features.description: Initialize the VS Code extension architecture, React frontend with esbuild, and two-way messaging between Extension Host and Webview.

features.name: harness-parser
features.title: Harness Data Parser
features.description: Implement the backend logic to parse .agents/agentic.json and subagent/skill Markdown files into a graph data model.

features.name: whiteboard-canvas
features.title: Read-Only Whiteboard Visualization
features.description: Integrate React Flow to visualize the Agents, Sub-agents, and Skills as an interactive graph within the Webview.

features.name: graph-editor
features.title: Visual Graph Editor & Write-back
features.description: Enable visual editing (creation/linking) of agents and skills with persistent updates back to the project files.

features.name: progress-timeline
features.title: SDD Progress Timeline
features.description: Visualize the SDD state machine and progress history in a git-style graphical timeline.

features.name: ui-ux-refinement
features.title: UI/UX Refinement & Layout Fixes
features.description: Ensure all agents/subagents are visible, fix detail panel visibility, and optimize whiteboard screen utilization.

features.name: subagent-skill-relationships
features.title: Subagent-Skill Relationships Visualization
features.description: Visualize which skills each subagent uses by adding skills array to agentic.json and ## Skills section to SUBAGENT.md files. Ensure subagents are positioned north of skills in the whiteboard layout.

features.name: ui-ux-visual-design
features.title: UI/UX Visual Design & Interaction Enhancement
features.description: Apply ui-ux-design-standards skill to improve node styling, edge clarity, spacing consistency, hover/selection feedback, empty states, and detail panel layout across the whiteboard and timeline.

features.name: relationship-editor-md-viewer-side-panel
features.title: Relationship Editor, MD Viewer & Side Panel for Entity Creation
features.description: Allow users to delete/modify edges between nodes, view SUBAGENT.md/SKILL.md content on node click, and open a side panel alongside the activity bar for creating entities with full Agent Skills spec fields.

features.name: semantic-skill-discovery
features.title: Semantic Skill Discovery & Suggestion
features.description: Infers subagent↔skill relationships from description texts using TF-IDF cosine similarity. Rendered as suggested edges on the whiteboard; user can accept to convert to hard-linked uses edges. Optional vscode.lm LLM re-ranking improves accuracy.

features.name: agent-skill-idoneity
features.title: Agent-Skill Idoneity & Semantic Ownership
features.description: Refactors subagent↔skill relationships to be driven by bidirectional semantic idoneity. Computes composite TF-IDF scores for all pairs, assigns a best semantic owner per skill, highlights mismatches where current uses differs from the best owner, and modulates edge styling by idoneity score.

features.name: enhanced-entity-intelligence
features.title: Enhanced Entity Discovery & Cross-Reference Intelligence
features.description: Improves subagent/skill detection and agent-skill relationship inference with full-body semantic signal (instead of only frontmatter description), cross-reference scanning from markdown bodies, name-boosted TF-IDF scoring, n-gram support for phrase-level matching, orphan subagent detection, and elimination of the arbitrary 500-char body truncation.

features.name: skill-toggle-and-suggestion-control
features.title: Skill Toggle & Suggestion Visibility Control
features.description: Allow users to permanently dismiss semantic relationship suggestions (they don't reappear after reload), and enable/disable (toggle) existing skill connections for agents and subagents. Also fixes critical bugs found by code analysis: suggestion persistence, double idoneity computation, implicit any types, and fragile event listeners.

features.name: open-file-in-editor
features.title: Open Markdown File in VS Code Editor
features.description: Fix the 'Open in Editor' button that was partially implemented in FEAT-009 but is effectively invisible. Root causes: icon-only button (codicon may not render in webview sandbox), placed inside tab content instead of the panel header, and shown for all node types including agent/feature which have no editable file. Fix: add a prominent text button in the detail panel header, visible only for skill and subagent nodes.

features.name: universal-agent-reader
features.title: Universal Agent Architecture Reader
features.description: Extend the plugin to detect and visualize agent architectures from any major agentic tool (Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, OpenCode, Windsurf, Continue) without requiring Harness SDD. Each tool's config is parsed through a dedicated adapter into the common HarnessGraph model, merged into a single unified whiteboard view.

features.name: relationship-visual-refinement
features.title: Relationship Lines Visual & Interaction Refinement
features.description: Improve how relationship edges look and interact with node boxes: per-type routing (smoothstep for manages, bezier for uses/suggested), per-type glow/shadow, label pills with edge color, hover/selection states per edge type, animated scroll for suggested edges, handle-pill visual integration, and Z-index layering for selected/hovered edges.

features.name: node-position-persistence-and-pill-linking
features.title: Node Position Persistence & Handle-Pill Linking
features.description: Fix whiteboard behavior where manually moved nodes snap back to auto-layout defaults after graph refreshes, and improve edge creation UX by allowing direct press-drag from visible handle pills to target node pills.

features.name: ci-github-actions
features.title: CI with GitHub Actions
features.description: Add a GitHub Actions workflow that, on every push and pull_request targeting main, runs `npm ci`, `npm run build`, `npm test`, and `./check.sh` to guarantee that PRs cannot merge with a broken build, broken tests, broken harness adapters, or a malformed feature list.

features.name: governance-docs
features.title: Populate Governance Documents (DESIGN.md, backlog.md, first ADR)
features.description: Replace the placeholder contents of DESIGN.md, progress/backlog.md, and progress/decisions.md with concrete, accurate content for the Harness Dashboard VS Code extension. Establish a real architectural overview, a prioritised backlog, and the first ADR documenting the project-level decision to use the Harness SDD framework itself. Closes the 'documentation says one thing, repo does another' gap identified in the 2026-06-10 project analysis.

features.name: vsix-gitignore-cleanup
features.title: Exclude *.vsix from git and clean the three committed binaries
features.description: Add `*.vsix` to `.gitignore`, remove the three committed VSIX binaries from the index with `git rm --cached`, and verify the CI workflow and the local `./check.sh` no longer see them as untracked noise. Trivial hygiene change. Closes the first P0 item in the backlog written by FEAT-019.

features.name: e2e-integration-test
features.title: End-to-end integration test of the critical user path
features.description: Add a `@vscode/test-electron` integration test that runs the extension in a headless VS Code instance, opens a fixture Harness SDD workspace, exercises the critical user path (activate → whiteboard renders → click a node → detail panel shows MD content → click 'Open in editor' → file opens in VS Code's text editor), and asserts the on-disk and on-screen state at each step. Closes the 'zero integration tests' gap noted in the 2026-06-10 analysis: the project ships 126 unit tests but no test that exercises the webview in a real VS Code instance.

features.name: repo-rename-decision
features.title: Decide the repository rename (harness-manager → harness-dashboard) and document as ADR-002
features.description: The repository, the npm package, the VSIX, and the README all reference 'harness-dashboard' except the GitHub repo name itself, which is still 'harness-manager'. This is the last P0 item in the backlog. The feature records the decision as ADR-002 (accept the mismatch, or rename the repo) and, if the rename is chosen, executes it. Closes the 'documented inconsistency' gap that has been visible since the project started.

features.name: adapter-config-paths-and-kiro-and-whiteboard-polish
features.title: Configurable adapter paths + Kiro adapter + whiteboard UX polish (animations, no overlap)
features.description: Three-part feature: (1) introduce a general 'Configuration Registry' pattern that lets the user override the detection paths of any adapter via VS Code settings (extensible, not Kiro-specific), so users with custom installs (e.g., a Kiro IDE install in a non-standard path) can still see their agents on the whiteboard; (2) add a Kiro adapter (detection file: .kiro/) as the FIRST consumer of the Configuration Registry; (3) polish the whiteboard UX: smoother, more professional node animations, and an explicit no-overlap guarantee for node positions. The Configuration Registry is the lasting architectural contribution; the Kiro adapter is its first payoff; the whiteboard polish closes two long-standing UX gaps.

features.name: steering-and-hooks-observability
features.title: Steering & Hooks Observability — visualise agent/file relationships on the whiteboard
features.description: Add 'steering' and 'hook' as new node types on the whiteboard. Parse agentic.json#steering[] and agentic.json#hooks[] resources, read the actual steering markdown files and hook scripts, and create edges showing their relationships: steering → subagent (based on applies_to), hook → agent. Each steering file and hook script gets a distinct visual style (icon, colour, shape) so users can see their full agentic workflow at a glance.

features.name: enhanced-sdd-panel
features.title: Enhanced SDD Panel — spec viewer, editor, and AI-assisted feature initiation
features.description: Create a dedicated SDD management panel alongside the whiteboard. Display spec files (requirements.md, design.md, tasks.md) for each feature in feature_list.json, show their chronology/status with visual state badges, allow editing spec files inline, and support initiating new specs directly from the panel. Integrate with the VS Code LLM API (vscode.lm) to assist in writing spec content — similar to how the Git extension generates commit messages — so users can generate EARS requirements, design decisions, or task lists with one click.

features.name: cross-framework-hooks-steering
features.title: Cross-framework hooks & steering discovery (Kiro, Claude Code, etc.)
features.description: Closes the FEAT-024 gap: projects that use Kiro, Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini, Copilot, or Windsurf — without Harness SDD's agentic.json — can have hooks and steering files under their framework root (e.g., .kiro/hooks/) or even the project root (e.g., hooks/). Add a discovery layer that scans both locations, deduplicates, creates hook/steering nodes, and wires them to the adapter's root agent and subagents. Per-adapter settings (hooksPath, steeringPath, discoverHooks, discoverSteering) and global settings (rootHooks, rootSteering) make the behaviour fully configurable. Backward-compatible with FEAT-024's Harness-SDD handling.

features.name: code-quality-on-save-hooks
features.title: Code Quality On-Save Hooks (KISS + DRY) — enforce principles from the steering files
features.description: Two hooks that run on every TypeScript save in src/ and enforce the KISS and DRY principles documented in steering/kiss-principle.md and steering/dry-principle.md. The KISS hook checks for overengineering: long files (>400 lines), long functions (>80 lines), deep nesting (>4 levels), unused parameters, and excessive swallowed exceptions. The DRY hook checks for duplication: repeated string literals (>=12 chars, 3+ times), repeated magic numbers, near-duplicate functions (Jaccard >= 0.85), and duplicate interface/type definitions. Each hook is its own bash + python3 script, registered in agentic.json#hooks[] under events on_file_saved_kiss and on_file_saved_dry, and reports issues to VS Code's Problems panel and an OutputChannel. A single JSON catalog (hooks/code-quality-checks.json) lists the rules and serves as the runtime form of the steering principles. Configurable via 5 settings: verifyOnSave, blockOnSave, kissEnabled, dryEnabled, severity. Plus a manual command to verify a chosen file.

features.name: universal-ai-provider
features.title: Universal AI Provider — configurable LM fallback for any IDE
features.description: Extends the AI generation system with a provider chain: vscode.lm first, then a configurable OpenAI-compatible API as fallback. Makes AI features work in any IDE (VS Code + Copilot, Kiro, VS Code without Copilot) without changing the webview. Adds harness-dashboard.checkLM command for diagnostics and 3 new settings (apiKey, endpoint, model). Zero new npm dependencies.
